title: Backfill embeddings for pre-T-080 L2 rows (one-shot migration so semantic dedup can see history)
current_state: T-080 added embedding-based semantic dedup at L2 write time. Embeddings are computed and stored only at the moment of write. The ~35 L2 rows that already existed before T-080 landed have no stored embedding â€” they are invisible to the dedup pipeline. Today's writes can dedup against today's writes, but not against the historical corpus. Until backfilled, dedup is partially blind.
structural_problem: Schema migrations that add a computed column should backfill the column for existing rows in the same release. T-080 added the column without backfill. Easy to forget for a long time because dedup *appears* to work fine.
target_state: scripts/backfill_l2_embeddings.py reads every L2 row missing an embedding, computes one with the same model T-080 uses, writes it back. Idempotent â€” re-running skips rows that already have embeddings. Logs progress. Can be killed and resumed.
files_affected: tools/tools_memory.py (no change required if it already exposes a write-embedding-only method; otherwise add one)
success_criteria: Running `python scripts/backfill_l2_embeddings.py` once embeds every L2 row that currently lacks an embedding
Idempotent: second run reports '0 rows needed embedding'
Resumable: can be killed mid-run, restarted, picks up where it left off
Logs to stdout: row count processed / skipped / failed
Smoke test after run: a known historical fact triggers dedup against a new near-duplicate write
risk_notes: Backfill burns embedding API calls (~35 rows, trivial cost). No risk to live L1/L3. Backup L2 table to data/l2_backup_pre_backfill.sql before running, just in case.
migration_plan: 1. Read T-080 / S-NNN solution entry to confirm which embedding model + dimensionality was chosen
2. Write scripts/backfill_l2_embeddings.py: query L2 rows WHERE embedding IS NULL, batch into groups of 10, compute embeddings, write back
3. Add `--dry-run` flag that reports count without writing
4. Run dry-run first; verify count matches expectation (~35)
5. Take L2 backup: pg_dump or Supabase export of organized_memory table to data/l2_backup_pre_backfill.sql
6. Run backfill for real. Capture output to logs/backfill_l2_embeddings.log
7. Smoke test: write a near-duplicate of a known pre-T-080 fact, verify dedup catches it
resolution: scripts/backfill_l2_embeddings.py created. Reads organized_memory rows WHERE embedding IS NULL (in content.metadata.embedding), computes Gemini embedding via memory.semantic_dedup.get_embedding, writes back. --dry-run flag for safe preview. Idempotent and resumable. verify.py PASS.
